server.name: jacs-mcp
server.title: JACS MCP Server
server.instructions: This MCP server provides data provenance and cryptographic signing for signed documents, agreements, A2A artifacts, agents, and configuration. Document signing: jacs_sign_document (sign JSON content), jacs_verify_document (verify a signed JACS document). Agent management: jacs_create_agent (create new agent with keys), jacs_reencrypt_key (rotate private key password). A2A artifacts: jacs_wrap_a2a_artifact (sign artifact with provenance), jacs_verify_a2a_artifact (verify wrapped artifact), jacs_assess_a2a_agent (assess remote agent trust level). A2A discovery: jacs_export_agent_card (export Agent Card), jacs_generate_well_known (generate .well-known documents), jacs_export_agent (export full agent JSON). Trust store: jacs_trust_agent (add agent to trust store), jacs_untrust_agent (remove from trust store, requires JACS_MCP_ALLOW_UNTRUST=true), jacs_list_trusted_agents (list all trusted agent IDs), jacs_is_trusted (check if agent is trusted), jacs_get_trusted_agent (get trusted agent JSON). Attestation: jacs_attest_create (create signed attestation with claims), jacs_attest_verify (verify attestation, optionally with evidence checks), jacs_attest_lift (lift signed document into attestation), jacs_attest_export_dsse (export attestation as DSSE envelope). Search: jacs_search (unified search across all signed documents). Inline text and media: jacs_sign_text (sign a markdown/text file in place), jacs_verify_text (verify inline JACS signatures), jacs_sign_image (sign PNG/JPEG/WebP by embedding metadata), jacs_verify_image (verify image signature), jacs_extract_media_signature (dump embedded JACS payload).

tools.name: jacs_assess_a2a_agent
tools.description: Assess the trust level of a remote A2A agent given its Agent Card. Applies a trust policy (open, verified, or strict) and returns whether the agent is allowed and at what trust level.

input_schema.description: Parameters for assessing trust level of a remote A2A agent.

agent_card_json.description: The A2A Agent Card JSON of the remote agent to assess

policy.description: Trust policy: 'open' (accept all), 'verified' (require JACS), or 'strict' (require trust store)

input_schema.title: AssessA2aAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_create
tools.description: Create a signed attestation document. Provide a JSON string with: subject (type, id, digests), claims (name, value, confidence, assuranceLevel), and optional evidence, derivation, and policyContext. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for creating an attestation.

params_json.description: JSON string containing attestation parameters: { subject: { type, id, digests }, claims: [{ name, value, confidence?, assuranceLevel? }], evidence?: [...], derivation?: {...}, policyContext?: {...} }

input_schema.title: AttestCreateParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_export_dsse
tools.description: Export an attestation as a DSSE envelope for in-toto/SLSA compatibility. Provide the signed attestation document JSON. Returns a DSSE envelope with payloadType, payload, and signatures. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting an attestation as a DSSE envelope.

attestation_json.description: JSON string of the signed attestation document to export as DSSE

input_schema.title: AttestExportDsseParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_lift
tools.description: Lift an existing signed JACS document into an attestation. Provide the signed document JSON and a JSON array of claims to attach. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for lifting a signed document to an attestation.

claims_json.description: JSON array of claim objects: [{ name, value, confidence?, assuranceLevel? }]

signed_doc_json.description: JSON string of the existing signed JACS document to lift

input_schema.title: AttestLiftParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_verify
tools.description: Verify an attestation document. Provide a document_key in 'jacsId:jacsVersion' format. Set full=true for full-tier verification including evidence and derivation chain checks. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying an attestation.

full.description: Set to true for full-tier verification (evidence + chain checks)

input_schema.title: AttestVerifyParams

tools.name: jacs_check_agreement
tools.description: Check the status of an agreement: how many agents have signed, whether quorum is met, whether it has expired, and which agents still need to sign. Use this to decide whether an agreement is complete and ready to act on.

input_schema.description: Parameters for checking agreement status.

signed_agreement.description: The agreement JSON to check status of

input_schema.title: CheckAgreementParams

tools.name: jacs_create_agent
tools.description: Create a new JACS agent with cryptographic keys. This is the programmatic equivalent of 'jacs create'. Returns agent ID and key paths. SECURITY: Requires JACS_MCP_ALLOW_REGISTRATION=true environment variable.

input_schema.description: Parameters for creating a new JACS agent programmatically.

password.description: Password for encrypting the private key. Must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

input_schema.title: CreateAgentProgrammaticParams

tools.name: jacs_create_agreement
tools.description: Create a multi-party cryptographic agreement. Use this when multiple agents need to formally agree on something -- like approving a deployment, authorizing a data transfer, or ratifying a decision. You specify which agents must sign, an optional quorum (e.g., 2-of-3), a timeout deadline, and algorithm constraints. Returns a signed agreement document to pass to other agents for co-signing.

input_schema.description: Parameters for creating a multi-party agreement.

agent_ids.description: List of agent IDs (UUIDs) that are parties to this agreement

document.description: JSON document that all parties will agree to. Can be any valid JSON object.

question.description: Question for signers, e.g. 'Do you approve deploying model v2?'

quorum.description: Minimum signatures required (M-of-N). If omitted, all agents must sign.

required_algorithms.description: Only allow these signing algorithms. Values: 'ring-Ed25519', 'pq2025'

timeout.description: ISO 8601 deadline after which the agreement expires. Example: '2025-12-31T23:59:59Z'

input_schema.title: CreateAgreementParams

tools.name: jacs_export_agent
tools.description: Export the local agent's full JACS JSON document. This includes the agent's identity, public key hash, and signed metadata.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting the local agent's full JSON document (no params needed).
input_schema.title: ExportAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_export_agent_card
tools.description: Export this agent's A2A Agent Card as JSON. The Agent Card describes the agent's capabilities, endpoints, and identity for A2A discovery.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting the local agent's A2A Agent Card (no params needed).
input_schema.title: ExportAgentCardParams

tools.name: jacs_extract_media_signature
tools.description: Extract the JACS signature payload embedded in a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image. By default returns the decoded JACS signed-document JSON string. Pass `raw_payload: true` to return the base64url wire form instead (useful for byte-for-byte relay or fuzz fixtures). Pass `robust: true` to fall back to LSB scan when the metadata channel has no payload (R-011; mirrors verify_image --robust). Returns `present: false` if no payload is found.

input_schema.description: Parameters for `jacs_extract_media_signature` (PRD §3.2).

file_path.description: Path to a signed image to extract the embedded JACS payload from

raw_payload.description: Return the raw base64url wire form instead of the decoded JACS signed-document JSON (default: false)

robust.description: Scan the LSB channel as a fallback if the metadata channel has no payload (default: false; cost: full pixel decode)

input_schema.title: ExtractMediaSignatureParams

tools.name: jacs_generate_well_known
tools.description: Generate all .well-known documents for A2A discovery. Returns an array of {path, document} objects that can be served at each path for agent discovery.

input_schema.description: Parameters for generating well-known documents.
input_schema.title: GenerateWellKnownParams

tools.name: jacs_get_trusted_agent
tools.description: Retrieve the full agent JSON document for a trusted agent from the local trust store. Fails if the agent is not trusted.

input_schema.description: Parameters for getting a trusted agent's details.

agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID (UUID format) to retrieve from the trust store

input_schema.title: GetTrustedAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_is_trusted
tools.description: Check whether a specific agent is in the local trust store. Returns a boolean indicating trust status.

input_schema.description: Parameters for checking if an agent is trusted.

agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID (UUID format) to check trust status for

input_schema.title: IsTrustedParams

tools.name: jacs_list_trusted_agents
tools.description: List all agent IDs currently in the local trust store. Returns the count and a list of trusted agent IDs.

input_schema.description: Parameters for listing trusted agents (no parameters required).
input_schema.title: ListTrustedAgentsParams

tools.name: jacs_reencrypt_key
tools.description: Re-encrypt the agent's private key with a new password. Use this to rotate the password protecting the private key without changing the key itself.

input_schema.description: Parameters for re-encrypting the agent's private key.

new_password.description: New password. Must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

old_password.description: Current password for the private key

input_schema.title: ReencryptKeyParams

tools.name: jacs_rotate_keys
tools.description: Rotate the agent's cryptographic keys. Generates a new keypair, signs a transition proof with the old key, re-signs the agent document and config with the new key. Optionally change the signing algorithm.

input_schema.description: Parameters for rotating the agent's cryptographic keys.

algorithm.description: Signing algorithm for the new keys (ring-Ed25519, pq2025). If omitted, keeps the current algorithm.

input_schema.title: RotateKeysParams

tools.name: jacs_search
tools.description: Search across all signed documents using the unified search interface. Supports fulltext search with optional filtering by document type. Results include relevance scores and matching snippets. The search method (fulltext, vector, or hybrid) is chosen automatically based on the storage backend.

SearchFieldFilter.description: Field-level filter accepted by the MCP search tool.

value.description: Exact value the field must match.

input_schema.description: Parameters for the unified search tool.

agent_id.description: Optional filter by signing agent ID

field_filter.description: Optional JSON field filter for exact matches

jacs_type.description: Optional filter by JACS document type (e.g., 'agreement', 'agent')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20)

min_score.description: Optional minimum score threshold between 0.0 and 1.0

query.description: Search query string to match against document content and names

input_schema.title: SearchParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_agreement
tools.description: Co-sign an existing agreement. Use this after receiving an agreement document from another agent. Your cryptographic signature is added to the agreement. The updated document can then be passed to the next signer or checked for completion.

input_schema.description: Parameters for signing an existing agreement.

signed_agreement.description: The full agreement JSON to sign. Obtained from jacs_create_agreement or from another agent.

input_schema.title: SignAgreementParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_document
tools.description: Sign arbitrary JSON content to create a cryptographically signed JACS document. Use this for attestation -- when you want to prove that content was signed by this agent. Returns the signed envelope with hash and document ID.

input_schema.description: Parameters for signing arbitrary content as a JACS document.

content.description: The JSON content to sign as a JACS document

input_schema.title: SignDocumentParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_image
tools.description: Sign a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image by embedding a JACS signature into format-native metadata (PNG iTXt, JPEG APP11, WebP XMP). Default mode is metadata-only and does NOT modify pixel data. Robust mode (PNG/JPEG only) additionally embeds into the LSB channel for re-encode survivability — pass `robust: true` to enable. Use `refuse_overwrite: true` to refuse an input that already carries a JACS signature (PRD §4.2.2 single-signer guard).

input_schema.description: Parameters for `jacs_sign_image`.

input_path.description: Path to the input image (PNG, JPEG, or WebP)

output_path.description: Path to write the signed output image

refuse_overwrite.description: Refuse to overwrite an input that already carries a JACS signature (PRD §4.2.2 single-signer; default: false)

robust.description: Embed signature into the LSB channel as well as metadata for re-encode survivability (default: false)

input_schema.title: SignImageParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_text
tools.description: Sign a text/markdown file in place by appending an inline JACS signature block. Content bytes are preserved exactly; the signature block is appended at the end of the file (PRD §4.1, C2). A `<path>.bak` is created by default before signing. Use this for signing markdown documents, agent prompts, or any plaintext file where the signature should be embedded in the file itself.

input_schema.description: Parameters for `jacs_sign_text` (PRD §4.1.1).

file_path.description: Path to the text/markdown file to sign in place

input_schema.title: SignTextParams

tools.name: jacs_trust_agent
tools.description: Add an agent to the local trust store. The agent's self-signature is cryptographically verified before it is trusted. Pass the full agent JSON document. Returns the trusted agent ID on success.

input_schema.description: Parameters for adding an agent to the local trust store.

agent_json.description: The full JACS agent JSON document to add to the trust store

input_schema.title: TrustAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_untrust_agent
tools.description: Remove an agent from the local trust store. SECURITY: Requires JACS_MCP_ALLOW_UNTRUST=true environment variable to prevent prompt injection attacks from removing trusted agents without user consent.

input_schema.description: Parameters for removing an agent from the trust store.

agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID (UUID format) to remove from the trust store

input_schema.title: UntrustAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_a2a_artifact
tools.description: Verify a JACS-wrapped A2A artifact. Checks the cryptographic signature and hash to confirm the artifact was signed by the claimed agent and has not been tampered with.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying a JACS-wrapped A2A artifact.

wrapped_artifact.description: The JACS-wrapped A2A artifact JSON to verify

input_schema.title: VerifyA2aArtifactParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_document
tools.description: Verify a signed JACS document given its full JSON string. Checks both the content hash and cryptographic signature. Use this when you have a signed document in memory (e.g. from an approval context or signed payload) and need to confirm its integrity and authenticity.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying a raw signed JACS document string.

document.description: The full signed JACS document JSON string to verify

input_schema.title: VerifyDocumentParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_image
tools.description: Verify a JACS signature embedded in a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image. Permissive by default (C1): missing signature returns `status: "missing_signature"` with `success: true`. With `strict: true`, missing signatures cause `success: false`. Pass `key_dir` for offline verification (PRD §4.1.5). Pass `robust: true` to also scan the LSB channel for a robust-mode payload (PRD §4.2.4) when no metadata payload is found.

input_schema.description: Parameters for `jacs_verify_image` (PRD §4.1.5, §4.2.4, C1).

file_path.description: Path to the signed image to verify

key_dir.description: Optional path to a directory containing public-key PEM files (PRD §4.1.5)

robust.description: Scan LSB channel for robust-mode payload when metadata is absent (default: false)

strict.description: Treat 'no signature found' as a real error instead of a typed status (default: false)

input_schema.title: VerifyImageParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_text
tools.description: Verify inline JACS signatures embedded in a text/markdown file. By default (permissive mode, C1), a file with no signature returns `status: "missing_signature"` with `success: true`. With `strict: true`, missing signatures cause `success: false` with an explicit error. Pass `key_dir` (PRD §4.1.5) for offline verification using pre-shared `<signer_id>.public.pem` files.

input_schema.description: Parameters for `jacs_verify_text` (PRD §4.1.5, C1).

file_path.description: Path to the signed text/markdown file to verify

key_dir.description: Optional path to a directory containing public-key PEM files for offline verification

strict.description: Treat 'no signature found' as a real error instead of a typed status (default: false)

input_schema.title: VerifyTextParams

tools.name: jacs_wrap_a2a_artifact
tools.description: Wrap an A2A artifact with JACS provenance. Signs the artifact JSON, binding this agent's identity to the content. Optionally include parent signatures for chain-of-custody provenance.

input_schema.description: Parameters for wrapping an A2A artifact with JACS provenance.

artifact_json.description: The A2A artifact JSON content to wrap with JACS provenance

parent_signatures.description: Optional JSON array of parent signatures for chain-of-custody provenance

input_schema.title: WrapA2aArtifactParams
